About

10 years on the
sales floor. Now building
the systems behind it.

I'm Giovanna, a Revenue Operations professional with 10 years of experience in B2B sales and commercial management. I spent a decade managing pipelines, teams, and client relationships in high-pressure sales environments.

Over time I became increasingly fascinated by something most sales organizations struggle with quietly: the gap between how revenue is supposed to work and how it actually works on the ground.

Reps who don't update the CRM because it doesn't help them. Pipelines full of deals that should have been closed months ago. Forecasts that leadership doesn't fully trust. These aren't people problems. They're system design problems.

That realization led me to Revenue Operations. I wanted to use everything I learned on the front line to build systems that actually support how sales teams work, not just track what they do.

HubSpot Certified Pipeline Architecture GTM Strategy CRM Design BDR Enablement French · English · Spanish Paris-based · Remote-ready
"Build the right system first. Then let the data show you what's inside it."
Core philosophy
"Revenue systems should be built for decision-making not just tracking."
Design principle

Currently open to Revenue Operations and Sales Operations roles in SaaS and Fintech scale-ups in France (Paris area) or remote.

Design Principles

How I think about
revenue systems

01
Capture decision signals, not just activity
02
Align CRM structure with the sales process
03
Make the CRM valuable for the sales team first
04
Enforce pipeline discipline through system design
05
Turn data into diagnostic visibility for leadership
Revenue Decision Framework

Five signal types.
One decision system.

A well-designed CRM structures the data needed to determine whether opportunities are real, progressing, or at risk. Every case study in this portfolio connects to one of these signal types.

i
Qualification
Is this a real opportunity worth AE time? ICP fit, pain confirmation, decision-maker identified.
ii
Momentum
Is the deal actually progressing? Next steps, stage aging, governance flags.
iii
Engagement
Is the buyer actively engaged? CRM adoption, activity signals, rep workflow alignment.
iv
Speed
Are we responding fast enough? SLA enforcement, routing logic, response time metrics.
v
Funnel
What does the full picture tell us? Conversion rates, pipeline coverage, forecast integrity.
System Architecture

Five layers.
One connected system.

The HubSpot sandbox simulates a SaaS revenue environment. Each layer feeds the next from first signal to revenue decision.

→
Layer 01
Lead Capture
Inbound & outbound motions, ICP targeting, intent signals
→
Layer 02
Lead Qualification
MQL routing, data gates, SQL bridge, SLA enforcement
→
Layer 03
Opportunity Pipeline
Stage gates, deal creation, AE assignment, progression signals
→
Layer 04
Pipeline Governance
Stalled deal flags, next step discipline, automated governance
Layer 05
Revenue Dashboards
Funnel health, execution discipline, forecast integrity

Case Study 01 Engagement Signals HubSpot Sandbox · HR Tech SaaS

CRM as a Rep Decision Tool

How CRM design makes adoption a natural byproduct of rep workflow, not a compliance requirement.

The Problem

In high-volume B2B sales environments, CRM updates happen reactively, before pipeline reviews, not during actual selling. Reps log activities to satisfy management reporting, not because the system helps them work better.

This reveals a design problem: the CRM was built for management visibility, not rep workflow. The result is a system that captures activity volume but misses the signals that actually matter: whether a deal is alive, whether the buyer is engaged, and what needs to happen next.

When reps don't trust the CRM to help them, they stop feeding it accurate data. Engagement signals become unreliable. Pipeline reviews turn into guesswork. The problem compounds: leadership pushes for more CRM discipline, reps comply minimally, data quality stays low, and trust in the system erodes further.

Pipeline Structure
Pipeline stages in HubSpot. Seven stages from Discovery to Closed Lost, each with calibrated close probabilities and conditional stage properties enforcing qualification signals at key transitions.
System Design

The redesign starts from a single question: what would make a rep open the CRM first thing in the morning, not because they have to, but because it helps them plan their day?

Automatic activity logging
Calls, emails, and meetings are captured automatically where possible, reducing manual entry. Reps spend time on outcomes, not data entry.
Mandatory meeting outcomes
Every logged meeting requires an outcome: Held, Rescheduled, or No-Show. This single field transforms activity data into an engagement signal. Leadership can now see not just how many meetings happened, but whether buyers are showing up.
Required next-step fields
Every open deal must carry a next step and a due date. No next step triggers a governance flag. This keeps deals moving and gives reps a built-in daily priority list.
Rep-facing dashboard
A dedicated dashboard shows each rep their own pipeline health: deals missing next steps, upcoming due dates, and engagement recency. Designed for daily use, not weekly reporting.
The Decision Enabled

Reps no longer update the CRM for management. They use it to plan their day. Meeting outcomes and next steps are captured in real time because doing so makes the rep's own work easier. For sales leadership, engagement signals become reliable for the first time: which buyers are showing up, which deals have clear momentum, and which ones are quietly dying. Coaching shifts from "why isn't the CRM updated?" to "this deal has had no buyer contact in 12 days. What's the plan?"

V2 Planned

Rep-facing deal prioritization view surfacing deals by next step due date and activity recency. Missed next step detection report flagging deals where the due date passed with no logged activity, surfaced automatically for manager review.

↑ Back to case studies
Case Study 02 Momentum Signals HubSpot Sandbox · HR Tech SaaS

Pipeline Governance

How stage rules, aging controls, and automated governance prevent stalled deals and restore pipeline trust.

The Problem

In most sales environments, pipelines grow in one direction: deals go in, but they don't always come out. Opportunities stay open long past the point of relevance. Closing a deal feels like failure, because the next review is tomorrow, because nobody wants to be the one who killed a potential win.

When the CRM has no mechanism to detect stalled momentum, deals survive on optimism rather than evidence. A pipeline full of stalled deals creates three compounding problems: forecast accuracy collapses, AE time gets diluted by zombie deals, and trust in the system erodes to the point where reps and managers stop using it to make decisions.

System Design
Stage definitions tied to qualification criteria
Each pipeline stage has a clear definition of what must be true for a deal to belong there. Moving a deal forward requires evidence, not optimism.
Stage aging monitoring
Every deal is tracked against a maximum time allowed per stage: Discovery 14 days, Qualified 21 days, Solution Fit 21 days. Exceeding the SLA triggers an automatic flag, not a close.
Stalled deal governance workflow
A daily automated check identifies deals with no next step date or time in stage exceeding the SLA. Flagged deals trigger an AE task and a manager notification. Governance status is set to "Flagged" immediately in pipeline views.
Governance reset mechanism
When the AE resolves the issue, the governance flag resets automatically to "OK". Dashboards stay accurate without manual cleanup.
Workflow in HubSpot
Stalled Deal Governance workflow. Daily trigger checks all active pipeline deals. Branches on missing next step or stage aging breach. Flags deals, creates AE tasks, notifies manager. Escalates to "Stalled" if unresolved after 3 business days.
The Decision Enabled

Pipeline reviews transform from a full-pipeline walkthrough into a focused conversation: "We have 4 flagged deals this week. Let's decide what happens to each one." Leadership can now distinguish between pipeline that is real and pipeline that is historical. For AEs, a governance flag is not a punishment. It is a prompt: move it forward, get a next step, or close it.

↑ Back to case studies
Case Study 03 Speed Signals HubSpot Sandbox · HR Tech SaaS

Speed-to-Lead Automation

How response SLAs and automated lead routing turn response speed from an assumption into a managed operational metric.

The Problem

High-intent leads don't fail because of bad salespeople. They fail because of bad handoffs. A prospect requests a demo. The form submission lands in a shared inbox. Someone sees it, eventually. By the time a rep makes contact, the buyer has already talked to a competitor, lost urgency, or simply moved on.

When lead routing depends on manual attention, response speed becomes a function of who happens to check their inbox first, not a managed operational metric. The gap between lead creation and first contact is rarely tracked. And what isn't tracked isn't managed.

MQL Routing Workflow
MQL Routing workflow. On MQL status change, the system checks for company employee band. Missing data pauses routing and creates a "Fix segmentation data" task. Valid data triggers round-robin assignment within the correct SDR segment.
SLA Enforcement Workflow
SLA Enforcement workflow. After assignment, a 1 business day delay fires a branch check on last activity date. No activity marks the contact as SLA breached, sends an in-app alert to the SDR, and escalates to manager if still untouched after a second day.
SQL Bridge · Opportunity Creation
SQL Bridge workflow. Triggered when ICP Fit, Primary Pain, and Decision Maker Identified are all confirmed. Checks for existing open deals on the company (company lock). If none exist, creates the deal and sets RM - Open Deal Exists to Yes.
System Design Summary
Automated routing with data gate
Employee band determines SDR segment. Missing data pauses routing and creates a fix task. No bad data enters the pipeline.
Round-robin assignment within segments
Every SDR gets a fair, balanced queue. No lead falls through because everyone assumed someone else would handle it.
24-hour SLA enforcement
After assignment, a business-day timer starts. No activity triggers an SDR alert and a high-priority task. Manager notified on breach.
SQL bridge with company lock
Once SDR confirms 3 SQL criteria, the system checks for an existing open deal. If none exists, it creates exactly one deal automatically. Prevents duplicate pipeline entries.
The Decision Enabled

Every high-intent lead gets a response within 24 hours or triggers an automatic escalation. Response time stops being a cultural expectation and becomes a system-enforced standard. For SDRs, instead of managing a shared inbox, they work a structured queue with clear priorities. For leadership, speed-to-lead becomes visible for the first time: which SDR is consistently breaching SLA, is response time improving or degrading?

V2 Planned

Business-hours SLA logic calculating response time within 09:00–18:00 only, to avoid false breach alerts triggered on leads assigned late Friday afternoon.

↑ Back to case studies
Case Study 04 Funnel Signals HubSpot Sandbox · HR Tech SaaS

Funnel Decision Dashboards

How three role-specific dashboards transform CRM data into the diagnostic signals revenue teams need to make better decisions, faster.

The Problem

Most CRM dashboards answer the wrong question. They show activity volume: calls made, emails sent, deals created. They tell you what your team did. What they rarely tell you is whether any of it is working, where opportunities are getting stuck, and what leadership should actually do differently next week.

A dashboard that tracks activity is a reporting tool. A dashboard that reveals patterns is a decision tool. The difference is not the data. it is the question the dashboard is designed to answer.

Without diagnostic visibility, revenue problems are discovered late. at the end of the quarter, when there is no time to course-correct. Every funnel problem has an early warning signal. The question is whether the system is designed to surface it.

Dashboard 1 · Funnel Health

For: Sales Leadership, SDR Manager · Frequency: Weekly · Question: Are marketing, SDRs, and AEs generating real opportunities?

Funnel Health dashboard. MQL-to-SQL conversion funnel by lifecycle stage, SQL Gate Quality tracking Discovery-to-Qualified progression, Win Rate by stage, and Time in Stage for all active pipeline stages.
Dashboard 2 · Execution Discipline

For: SDRs and AEs daily, Managers for coaching · Frequency: Daily · Question: Is the revenue system being executed correctly today?

Execution Discipline dashboard. Deals missing Next Step Date by AE, Governance Flag breakdown (OK vs Missing Next Step), MQL SLA Breaches by SDR, and Aging Breaches on open deals. all designed for daily use, not weekly reporting.
Dashboard 3 · Forecast Integrity

For: VP Sales, CRO · Frequency: Weekly / Monthly · Question: Can leadership trust the pipeline to predict revenue?

Forecast Integrity dashboard. Early pipeline coverage by deal count and stage, Budget Range Mix for qualified deals, Weighted Pipeline value for Proposal and Negotiation stages, and Total vs Weighted pipeline comparison to surface forecast reliability.
The Decision Enabled

Funnel reviews stop being status updates and become diagnostic sessions. Leadership arrives at the pipeline review already knowing where the problems are. The conversation shifts from "let's go through every deal" to "funnel conversion dropped between Qualified and Solution Fit this month. what changed?" For SDRs and AEs, the execution discipline dashboard creates daily clarity: a focused list of what needs action today, not a full pipeline review. For the VP Sales, the forecast integrity dashboard replaces gut feel with evidence.

V2 Planned

Discovery outcome tracking (Qualified / Disqualified / Nurture / No-show) to evaluate conversation quality, not just volume. Marketing attribution loop connecting campaigns to pipeline created to revenue closed.

Case Study 05 · Part 1 GTM & Lead Generation ✦ New

Designing a Signal-Based
Prospecting System

After conversations with RevOps managers and VP Sales, a pattern emerged: lead generation is where the GTM motion breaks down first. This case study documents how to design a prospecting system that goes beyond inbound and outbound into a third motion built on intent signals.

The Problem

Most B2B GTM teams operate on two motions: wait for inbound or push outbound. Both have structural limits. Inbound depends on marketing investment and brand maturity. Outbound at scale requires volume that dilutes quality. Neither is designed to respond to the moment a prospect is actively in a buying process.

Signal-based prospecting is the third motion. It uses behavioral and intent data to identify prospects who are in-market right now and builds the operational infrastructure to act on that signal before competitors do.

The Prospecting Flow
01
ICP Defined
02
Source Leads
03
Enrichment
04
CRM
05
Outreach
06
Intent Signals
07
BDR
3-Level Automation Stack

The automation stack is built in three levels according to team maturity and data quality. Each level unlocks the next. Starting at Level 3 without Level 1 working correctly is the most common and most expensive mistake.

L1
Level 1 · Automatic Enrichment
A contact enters the CRM. Their data is enriched automatically with no manual action.
Trigger
New Contact
Form / CSV / BDR import
→
Automation
Make detects
Webhook on HubSpot
→
Enrichment
Lusha / FullEnrich
Email + phone via API
→
CRM Update
HubSpot fields
Auto-populated
→
Output
Contact ready
BDR queue in seconds
Stack: HubSpot + Make + Lusha/FullEnrich · Simple stack, immediate impact.
L2
Level 2 · ICP-Based Extraction
Define the rules once. The tool scrapes sources continuously and feeds the CRM with no human intervention.
Trigger
ICP Profile set
Title, size, geo, sector
→
Scraping
Clay / FullEnrich
LinkedIn + B2B bases
→
Push to CRM
Make / N8N
Routed + enriched
→
Routing
HubSpot workflow
BDR assignment rules
→
Output
BDR queue
Qualified, ready to work
Stack: Clay/FullEnrich + Make/N8N + HubSpot · Relevant for international BDR teams at volume.
L3
Level 3 · Intent-Based Automation
A behavioral signal triggers the entire flow. Zero human intervention.
Intent Signal
Cognism Intent
Pricing visits / LegalTech search
→
Automation
Make triggered
Account created in HubSpot
→
Routing
BDR assigned
By country / segment
→
Sequence
LGM / Lemlist
Targeted sequence starts
→
Alert
Slack notification
BDR notified in real time
→
Output
Warm outreach
Active prospect contacted
Stack: Cognism Intent + Make + HubSpot + LGM/Lemlist + Slack · Evaluate against current stack maturity before deploying.
KPI Framework

Performance is tracked at 3 levels. If data quality is poor, BDRs underperform. If BDRs underperform, the pipeline is empty. The problem often looks commercial but starts with the tool.

Level 1 · Tool Performance
Is the stack delivering good data?
Enrichment Rate
% of imported contacts with valid email/phone
Target: >70%
Bounce Rate
% of emails returned as undeliverable. Domain reputation risk above 5%.
Target: <5%
Cost per Contact
Tool budget ÷ usable contacts. Real profitability comparison across tools.
Benchmark across stack
Level 2 · BDR Performance
Is the machine generating opportunities?
Speed-to-Lead
Time from lead arrival to first BDR contact. Conversion drops 80% after 5 min for hot inbounds.
Target: <5 min (inbound)
Reply Rate
% of outreach recipients who respond. Below 2% signals a messaging or targeting problem.
Benchmark: 5 to 15%
Meetings Booked
Qualified meetings per BDR per month. Gaps between reps often reveal tooling or ICP issues, not skill.
Track by BDR and market
Lead-to-MQL
% of leads that pass the marketing qualification filter. Low rate signals wrong ICP or overly strict criteria.
Watch for misalignment
Level 3 · Funnel Performance
Is it converting to revenue?
MQL to SQL
% of MQLs validated as real opportunities by BDRs. Low rate = Sales and Marketing misaligned on what a good lead looks like.
Classic alignment signal
SQL to Deal
% of SQLs that result in a proposal sent by AE. Low rate = BDRs passing leads too early, AEs losing time on unready prospects.
BDR handoff quality
Pipeline Velocity
Deals × Win Rate × Avg Deal Value ÷ Sales Cycle. Compares how fast each channel generates revenue, not just how much.
Channel investment signal
BDR Enablement Framework
Lead Assignment
Structured routing by ICP segment, geography, or account tier. BDRs receive prioritized queues, not undifferentiated lists. Signal strength determines queue position.
SLA & Qualification Standards
Response SLAs enforced by signal type: intent-triggered leads get a 4-hour SLA vs standard 24-hour. Qualification criteria standardized so SQL definition is consistent across BDRs.
BDR Performance Tracking
KPIs tracked at 3 levels: tool performance (data quality, enrichment rates), BDR performance (contact rate, SQL rate, SLA adherence), funnel performance (intent-to-SQL, pipeline sourced).
The Decision Enabled

BDR teams stop working from static lists and start working from live signal queues. Lead generation shifts from a volume game to a timing game: reaching the right prospect at the moment they are actively looking. The infrastructure makes that timing systematic, not accidental.

Case Study 05 · Part 2 AI in the Revenue Stack ✦ New

Using AI to Scale
Revenue Execution

AI doesn't replace revenue systems. it amplifies them. When the underlying data infrastructure is clean and structured, AI becomes a multiplier: scoring at scale, personalizing at speed, and surfacing insight no human could catch manually.

The Principle

Most conversations about AI in sales focus on tools. The right question is different: what does AI make possible once the system is built correctly?

AI applied to a broken pipeline gives you faster noise. AI applied to a clean, signal-based system gives you a compounding advantage, because every model improves as more structured data flows through it.

Three AI Applications
Scoring & Automatic Qualification
AI models trained on historical SQL and win data score incoming leads and flag high-probability contacts before BDR review. Qualification becomes a ranked recommendation, not a manual judgment call. Output: a daily ranked queue with confidence scores, surfacing the 20% of leads worth 80% of BDR attention.
Sequence Personalization at Scale
Dynamic variables populated from enrichment data create outreach that reflects each prospect's context: industry, tech stack, recent trigger event, company growth signals. BDRs send personalized sequences without writing them from scratch. Output: higher reply rates without linear increase in BDR time.
Qualitative Enrichment by Summary
LLM-generated briefing cards summarize prospect context from news, LinkedIn activity, and job postings. inserted directly into the CRM record before first contact. BDRs arrive informed, not just armed with a name and a job title. Output: first conversations that are relevant, not generic.
The Dependency Chain
1
Clean CRM data (structured signals, no noise)
2
Enrichment layer (firmographic + technographic + intent)
3
AI scoring, personalization, and enrichment summaries

AI is always layer 3. It amplifies what's already there. it doesn't replace what's missing.

The Decision Enabled

Revenue teams can operate at a scale that was previously impossible without headcount. The system identifies who to call, why now, and what to say. before the BDR picks up the phone. That's not automation replacing judgment. That's infrastructure enabling better judgment, faster.

↑ Back to case studies
System at a Glance

What the system solves.

Every layer of the revenue system addresses a specific operational risk.

Operational Risk System Mechanism Layer
AEs waste time on weak opportunities SQL gate ensures only qualified leads create deals Qualification
Pipeline inflation from duplicate or stale deals Company lock + stage aging governance Governance
High-intent leads fall through the cracks MQL routing + SLA enforcement Speed
Deals progress without evidence Stage gates capture key qualification signals Pipeline
Forecasts based on optimism Late stages require amount, close date, and next step Forecasting
BDRs work without signal context Intent-based lead prioritization and automation stack Lead Gen
